title: The Cape
hero_alt: The Cape — Cape Cod, by Peter de Lory
pullQuote: Photography is not about the thing photographed. It is about how that thing looks photographed.
quoteAttr: Garry Winogrand, quoted by Peter de Lory
body: Peter de Lory began The Cape in 1964, after leaving Cape Cod for college, and returned to it over the years through the early 1980s — aerials and landscapes of the place he is from. The touchstone is Edward Hopper: “that sense of simplify, and moment just frozen.” In his words, it is less a memoir than a look back to understand where he is from — “the feeling of being in this sparse, but somehow intimate land and seascape.”
